implemented_requirements.description: Add mobile hamburger menu to Header.tsx

files.changes: Added useState for mobile menu state, hamburger button visible on lg:hidden, mobile drawer with nav items, wishlist, cart, and auth buttons. Desktop nav hidden on mobile with hidden lg:flex.

implemented_requirements.description: Fix or remove broken footer links (About, Privacy, Terms, etc.)

files.changes: Replaced all broken links (/about, /story, /career, /team, /blog, /faq, /privacy, /terms) with 'Coming Soon' styled spans. Retained functional contact and social media links.
Added useState for email and status, validateEmail function, handleSubmit with form validation, success/error feedback messages, loading state, and form accessibility.
Changed mauve DEFAULT from #ad778d to #9a6680 to meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio with white text.

implemented_requirements.description: Standardize primary action color (use mauve consistently)

files.changes: Updated primary variant from bg-primary-600 to bg-mauve, updated focus:ring from primary-500 to mauve, updated outline focus ring to mauve.

implemented_requirements.notes: Header.tsx, ProductCard.tsx, and HeroBanner.tsx were already using mauve directly via Tailwind classes. Only Button.tsx needed updating to use mauve for primary variant.

files.changes: Hidden product images container on mobile (hidden lg:flex), made section min-h-[400px] with lg:h-[576px], made CTA button responsive (w-full sm:w-auto lg:w-[324px]), adjusted text sizes for mobile with responsive classes.

tests_needed: Footer.test.tsx: newsletter form validation and submission
accessibility_improvements: Header: Added aria-label and aria-expanded to hamburger button
Footer: Added aria-label to newsletter input, role=status and role=alert for feedback messages
All components: Maintained keyboard navigation and focus states
notes: Currency kept as INR (₹) per requirements - no changes to currency formatting
All broken links in footer replaced with 'Coming Soon' indicators
Newsletter form uses simulated API - will need backend integration when available
Mobile menu uses CSS-based drawer (no external libraries added)
